---
status: draft
shortDescription: "A concrete class only needs to implement abstract methods declared in its direct supertypes"
concepts:
  - Class
  - Method
  - Subtyping
  - Inheritance
related:
# - id: LiteralNoExpression
#   relation: Special case
inOtherPl:
# - pl: JavaScript
#   id: ConcreteClassOnlyImplementDirectAbstract
# - pl: Python
#   id: ConcreteClassOnlyImplementDirectAbstract
spec:
# - id: jls13
#   sections: ["11.8"]
plVersion:
# from: jls8
# to: jls14
---
A concrete class that implements an interface and/or extends an abstract class only needs to implement those abstract methods explicitly declared in the interface it implements and/or the abstract class it extends.

frontmatter.shortDescription: A concrete class only needs to implement abstract methods declared in its direct supertypes
